mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of months following rate increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns (positive direction in >50% of cases) or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output that boosts short-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of instances during recovery phases, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month CPI acceleration above 0.4% triggers inflation fears that discount future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation deterioration, which suppresses long-term growth expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns (positive direction in >50% of cases) or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 3.0% confirms robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following strong GDP prints, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains that delay earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months during recovery regimes, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline (t-stat < 1.5).
